nodes.title: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high e...
nodes.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

nodes.title: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on ...
nodes.description: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or interpretation.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or interpretation.

nodes.title: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing ...
nodes.description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.

nodes.title: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums (e
nodes.description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums (e.g., print or digital text, video, multimedia) to present a particular topic or idea.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums (e.g., print or digital text, video, multimedia) to present a particular topic or idea.

nodes.title: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the ...
nodes.description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints.

nodes.title: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including ...
nodes.description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.

nodes.title: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, includ...
nodes.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.

nodes.title: Analyze how a text makes connections among and distinctions between individua...
nodes.description: Analyze how a text makes connections among and distinctions between individuals, ideas, or events (e.g., through comparisons, analogies, or categories).
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Analyze how a text makes connections among and distinctions between individuals, ideas, or events (e.g., through comparisons, analogies, or categories).

nodes.title: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the cours...
nodes.description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to supporting ideas; provide an objective summary of the text.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to supporting ideas; provide an objective summary of the text.

nodes.title: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the...
nodes.description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

nodes.title: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dr...
nodes.description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

nodes.title: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or ...
nodes.description: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new.

nodes.title: Analyze the extent to which a filmed or live production of a story or drama s...
nodes.description: Analyze the extent to which a filmed or live production of a story or drama stays faithful to or departs from the text or script, evaluating the choices made by the director or actors.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Analyze the extent to which a filmed or live production of a story or drama stays faithful to or departs from the text or script, evaluating the choices made by the director or actors.

nodes.title: Analyze how differences in the points of view of the characters and the audie...
nodes.description: Analyze how differences in the points of view of the characters and the audience or reader (e.g., created through the use of dramatic irony) create such effects as suspense or humor.
